summaries.bn: ইমাম সাদিক (আ.) কবুতরকে নবীদের পছন্দের পাখি হিসেবে বর্ণনা করেছেন এবং জানিয়েছেন যে ঘরে কবুতর থাকলে জিনদের অনিষ্ট থেকে বাসিন্দারা রক্ষা পায়। বোকা জিনরা কবুতরের দিকে মনোযোগ দেয় বলে মানুষদের ছেড়ে দেয়। বর্ণনাকারী নিজেও ইমামের ঘরে তাঁর পুত্র ইসমাইলের জন্য কবুতর দেখেছেন।
summaries.de: Imam al-Sadiq (Friede sei mit ihm) beschreibt die Taube als einen Vogel, den die Propheten in ihren Häusern hielten, und erklärt, dass ihre Anwesenheit die Bewohner vor dem Treiben der Dschinnen schützt. Die unvernünftigen Dschinnen beschäftigen sich mit den Tauben und lassen die Menschen in Ruhe. Der Überlieferer bezeugt persönlich, dass der Imam Tauben für seinen Sohn Ismail in seinem Haus hatte.
summaries.en: Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) describes the pigeon as a bird traditionally kept by the Prophets in their homes, and explains that its presence in a house protects the inhabitants from harm by mischievous jinn. The jinn, being distracted by the pigeon, leave the human residents undisturbed. This teaching reflects a broader Shia understanding of the natural world as imbued with spiritual significance, where certain elements — like the pigeon — serve protective and blessed functions. The narrator corroborates this by noting that Imam al-Sadiq himself kept pigeons in his home for his son Ismail.
summaries.es: El Imam al-Sadiq (la paz sea con él) describe la paloma como un ave que los profetas guardaban en sus hogares, y explica que su presencia protege a los habitantes de las travesuras de los djinn. Los djinn insensatos se distraen con las palomas y dejan en paz a las personas. El narrador corrobora esto al ver personalmente palomas en el hogar del Imam para su hijo Ismail.
summaries.fa: امام صادق علیه السلام کبوتر را از پرندگان پیامبران معرفی می‌کند و می‌فرماید که وجود آن در خانه، ساکنین را از آزار جن حفظ می‌کند. جن‌های نابخرد که در خانه‌ها اذیت می‌کنند، با کبوتر سرگرم می‌شوند و مردم را رها می‌کنند. راوی نیز خود کبوتری در خانه امام برای پسرش اسماعیل دیده است.
summaries.fr: L'Imam al-Sadiq (que la paix soit sur lui) décrit le pigeon comme un oiseau que les prophètes élevaient dans leurs demeures, soulignant que sa présence protège les habitants des nuisances des djinns malveillants. Ces derniers, occupés à tourmenter les pigeons, laissent les humains en paix. Le narrateur témoigne personnellement avoir vu des pigeons dans le foyer de l'Imam pour son fils Ismail.
summaries.id: Imam al-Shadiq (alaihissalam) menjelaskan bahwa merpati adalah burung yang disukai para nabi dan dipelihara di rumah-rumah mereka. Kehadiran merpati di rumah melindungi penghuninya dari gangguan jin yang usil, karena jin-jin itu akan teralihkan perhatiannya kepada merpati dan membiarkan manusia. Perawi sendiri menyaksikan bahwa Imam memelihara merpati untuk putranya Ismail.
summaries.ru: Имам аль-Садик (мир ему) описывает голубя как птицу, которую пророки держали в своих домах, объясняя, что её присутствие защищает жильцов от вреда со стороны джиннов. Неразумные джинны, отвлекаясь на голубей, оставляют людей в покое. Передатчик лично засвидетельствовал, что у Имама в доме был голубь для его сына Исмаила.
summaries.tr: İmam Sadık (a.s.), güvercinin peygamberlerin evlerinde besledikleri mübarek kuşlardan olduğunu ve evde güvercin bulunmasının sakinleri cin şerrinden koruduğunu açıklamaktadır. Cinlerin beyinsizleri güvercinlerle meşgul olup insanları rahat bırakır. Ravi de İmam'ın evinde oğlu İsmail için bizzat bir güvercin gördüğünü aktarmaktadır.
summaries.ur: امام صادق علیہ السلام نے کبوتر کو انبیاء کے پرندوں میں شامل قرار دیا اور بتایا کہ گھر میں کبوتر ہونے سے جنوں کی آفت سے حفاظت ہوتی ہے۔ شریر جن کبوتر کے ساتھ مصروف ہو جاتے ہیں اور گھر والوں کو تنگ نہیں کرتے۔ راوی نے خود بھی امام کے گھر میں ان کے بیٹے اسماعیل کے لیے کبوتر دیکھا۔
summaries.zh: 伊玛目萨迪格（愿主赐他平安）描述鸽子是先知们在家中饲养的吉祥鸟，家中有鸽子便能保护居住者不受精灵的侵扰。愚蠢的精灵转而去戏弄鸽子，从而放过了家中的人。传述者亲眼看见伊玛目家中为其子伊斯玛仪养着鸽子，印证了这一教导。

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/468)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) الْحَمَامُ طَيْرٌ مِنْ طُيُورِ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ ( عليهم السلام ) الَّتِي كَانُوا يُمْسِكُونَ فِي بُيُوتِهِمْ وَ لَيْسَ مِنْ بَيْتٍ فِيهِ حَمَامٌ إِلَّا لَمْ تُصِبْ أَهْلَ ذَلِكَ الْبَيْتِ آفَةٌ مِنَ الْجِنِّ إِنَّ سُفَهَاءَ الْجِنِّ يَعْبَثُونَ فِي الْبَيْتِ فَيَعْبَثُونَ بِالْحَمَامِ وَ يَدَعُونَ النَّاسَ قَالَ فَرَأَيْتُ فِي بُيُوتِ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) حَمَاماً لِابْنِهِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Moala Bin Muhammad, from Al Washa, from a man, from Umar Bin Yazeed, from Abu Salma who said,

‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The pigeon is a bird from the birds of the Prophets<sup>as</sup> which they<sup>as</sup> were keeping in their<sup>as</sup> houses, and there is none from a house wherein is a pigeon, except that its inhabitants would not be hit by an affliction from the Jinn. The foolish ones from the Jinn are tampering in the house, so they would be tampering with the pigeons instead and leave the people’. He (the narrator) said, ‘So I saw in the rooms of Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, a pigeon for his<sup>asws</sup> son Ismail’.
en.sarwar: 8. Al-Husayn ibn Muhammad has narrated from Mu‘alla’ ibn Muhammad from al-Washsha’ from a man from ‘Umar ibn Yazid from abu Salamah who has said the following: “Abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that pigeons are of the birds of the Prophets, ‘Alayhim al- Salam, who kept them in their homes. Any home in which there is a pigeon, the people therein are not harmed by the dimwitted ones of Jinn who plays in the house, thus they play with the pigeons and leave the people alone.’ I then saw in the house of abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, a pigeon that belonged to his son ’Isma‘il.”

translations.bn: আবুল হাসান আলাইহিস সালাম বললেন, তাঁর ঘরের কবুতরের দিকে তাকিয়ে: কবুতরের এমন কোনো ঝাঁকুনি নেই যে সে ঝাঁকায়, কিন্তু আল্লাহ তার মাধ্যমে পৃথিবীর দৃঢ়প্রতিজ্ঞ দুষ্কর্মীদের মধ্যে যারা ঘরে প্রবেশ করে তাদের তাড়িয়ে দেন।
translations.de: Abu al-Hasan (Friede sei mit ihm) sagte, während er auf die Tauben in seinem Haus blickte: 'Es gibt kein Flattern, mit dem es flattert, außer dass Gott dadurch diejenigen vertreibt, die das Haus betreten wollen, von den entschlossenen Übeltätern der Erde.'
translations.en: Abu al-Hasan (peace be upon him) said, while looking at pigeons in his house: 'There is not a single flutter with which it flutters, except that God repels through it those who enter the house from among the determined evildoers of the earth.'
translations.es: Abu al-Hasan (la paz sea con él) dijo, mientras miraba a las palomas en su casa: 'No hay un solo aleteo con el que aletee, excepto que Dios aleja por medio de él a quienes entran a la casa de entre los determinados malhechores de la tierra.'
translations.fa: ابوالحسن علیه السلام فرمود، در حالی که به کبوتران خانه‌اش نگاه می‌کرد: هیچ تکانی نیست که کبوتر با آن تکان می‌خورد، مگر اینکه خداوند به وسیله آن، کسانی را که با عزم بد از اهل زمین به خانه وارد می‌شوند، دور می‌کند.
translations.fr: Abu al-Hasan (que la paix soit sur lui) dit, en regardant les pigeons dans sa maison : 'Il n'y a pas un seul frémissement avec lequel il frémit, sauf qu'Allah repousse par lui ceux qui entrent dans la maison parmi les déterminés malfaisants de la terre.'
translations.id: Abu al-Hasan (alaihis salam) bersabda sambil melihat merpati di rumahnya: 'Tidak ada satu pun kepakan yang ia kepakkan, melainkan Allah mengusir melaluinya orang-orang yang masuk ke rumah dari golongan orang-orang bertekad jahat di bumi.'
translations.ru: Абу аль-Хасан (мир ему) сказал, глядя на голубей в своём доме: 'Нет ни одного трепета, которым оно встряхивается, кроме как Аллах этим отгоняет тех, кто входит в дом из числа решительных злодеев земли.'
translations.tr: Ebu'l-Hasan (aleyhisselam) evindeki güvercinlere bakarak buyurdu: 'Güvercinin her çırpınışında Allah, yeryüzünün azimli kötülerinden evi girenlerden onu uzaklaştırır.'
translations.ur: ابو الحسن علیہ السلام نے فرمایا جبکہ انہوں نے اپنے گھر میں کبوتروں کی طرف دیکھا: کبوتر کی ہر پھڑپھڑاہٹ کے ساتھ اللہ تعالیٰ اس کے ذریعے زمین کے عزم والوں میں سے ان لوگوں کو بھگا دیتا ہے جو گھر میں داخل ہونے کی کوشش کرتے ہیں۔
translations.zh: 阿布·哈桑（愿主赐他平安）说，同时望着他家中的鸽子：「鸽子每一次颤动，真主都借此驱逐那些来自大地上坚定作恶者中试图进入房屋的人。」

diacritics_changes: Added full tashkeel throughout; the source text had partial/no diacritics

isnad_matn.isnad_ar: عِدَّةٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْقَاسِمِ بْنِ يَحْيَى عَنْ جَدِّهِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ رَاشِدٍ عَنْ يَعْقُوبَ بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ قَالَ
isnad_matn.matn_ar: قَالَ أَبُو الْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ وَنَظَرَ إِلَى حَمَامٍ فِي بَيْتِهِ مَا مِنِ انْتِفَاضٍ يَنْتَفِضُ بِهَا إِلَّا نَفَّرَ اللَّهُ بِهَا مَنْ دَخَلَ الْبَيْتَ مِنْ عُزْمَةِ أَهْلِ الْأَرْضِ

narrators.known_identity: Imam Musa al-Kazim (AS) or Imam Ali al-Ridha (AS)

key_phrases.phrase_en: the determined evildoers of the earth

en.انْتِفَاض: flutter, twitch, shaking movement (of a bird)
en.عُزْمَة: the determined/resolute ones; those with firm evil intentions
en.نَفَّرَ: repelled, drove away, caused to flee

seo_questions.bn: ইমাম আবুল হাসান ঘরে কবুতর পালনের বিষয়ে কী বলেছেন?
seo_questions.de: Was sagte Imam Abu al-Hasan über das Halten von Tauben zu Hause als Schutz?
seo_questions.en: What did Imam Abu al-Hasan say about keeping pigeons in the house for protection?
seo_questions.es: ¿Qué dijo el Imam Abu al-Hasan sobre criar palomas en casa como protección?
seo_questions.fa: امام ابوالحسن درباره نگهداری کبوتر در خانه چه فرمودند؟
seo_questions.fr: Qu'a dit l'Imam Abu al-Hasan sur l'élevage de pigeons à la maison pour la protection ?
seo_questions.id: Apa yang dikatakan Imam Abu al-Hasan tentang memelihara merpati di rumah?
seo_questions.ru: Что сказал Имам Абу аль-Хасан о содержании голубей дома для защиты?
seo_questions.tr: İmam Ebu'l-Hasan evde güvercin beslemek hakkında ne buyurdu?
seo_questions.ur: امام ابو الحسن نے گھر میں کبوتر پالنے کے بارے میں کیا فرمایا؟
seo_questions.zh: 伊玛目阿布·哈桑关于在家养鸽子作为保护说了什么？

similar_content_hints.description: Other hadiths in al-Kafi's chapter on pigeons discussing the blessings and spiritual benefits of keeping pigeons in the home.
Hadiths discussing animals kept in the home that offer protection from evil or jinn, such as certain birds or animals mentioned in Shia traditions.
Narrations from Imam al-Kazim or Imam al-Ridha about household practices that attract divine mercy or repel harm.

summaries.bn: ইমাম তাঁর ঘরের কবুতর দেখে শিক্ষা দিলেন যে প্রতিটি ঝাঁকুনি আল্লাহর সুরক্ষার মাধ্যম হিসেবে দুষ্কর্মীদের তাড়িয়ে দেয়। এই হাদিসটি ঘরে কবুতর পালনকে আধ্যাত্মিক সুরক্ষা হিসেবে উৎসাহিত করে।
summaries.de: Der Imam beobachtete die Tauben in seinem Haus und lehrte, dass jedes Flattern als göttlicher Schutz dient, der böswillige Eindringlinge vertreibt. Dieser Hadith spiegelt die traditionelle Praxis wider, Tauben als eine Form des spirituellen Schutzes des Hauses zu halten.
summaries.en: The Imam observed the pigeons kept in his house and taught that each flutter or movement of the pigeons serves as a divine protection, repelling malicious intruders or those with evil intentions. This hadith reflects the traditional practice of keeping pigeons as a form of spiritual protection in the home, a topic addressed in this chapter of al-Kafi. The original audience would have understood this as affirming that even the natural movements of birds kept in a pious household are instruments of divine blessing and protection.
summaries.es: El Imam observó las palomas en su casa y enseñó que cada aleteo sirve como protección divina, alejando a los malhechores. Este hadiz refleja la práctica tradicional de criar palomas como forma de protección espiritual del hogar.
summaries.fa: امام با نگاه به کبوتران خانه‌شان فرمودند که هر تکان خوردن کبوتر ابزاری است برای دور کردن افراد بدنیت از خانه. این روایت از فضیلت نگهداری کبوتر در خانه به عنوان حفاظت معنوی سخن می‌گوید.
summaries.fr: L'Imam observa les pigeons dans sa maison et enseigna que chaque battement d'ailes sert de protection divine, repoussant les malfaiteurs déterminés. Ce hadith reflète la pratique traditionnelle d'élever des pigeons comme forme de protection spirituelle du foyer.
summaries.id: Imam mengamati merpati-merpati di rumahnya dan mengajarkan bahwa setiap kepakan merpati berfungsi sebagai perlindungan ilahi yang mengusir orang-orang jahat. Hadis ini mencerminkan tradisi memelihara merpati sebagai bentuk perlindungan spiritual.
summaries.ru: Имам наблюдал за голубями в своём доме и учил, что каждый трепет их крыльев служит божественной защитой, отгоняющей злоумышленников. Этот хадис отражает традиционную практику содержания голубей как формы духовной защиты дома.
summaries.tr: İmam evindeki güvercinleri gözlemleyerek, her çırpınışlarının Allah tarafından kötü niyetli kişileri evden uzaklaştırmak için bir araç olarak kullanıldığını öğretti. Bu hadis, evcil güvercinlerin manevi koruma amacıyla beslenmesinin caiz olduğuna dair bir delil sayılmaktadır.
summaries.ur: امام نے اپنے گھر میں رکھے کبوتروں کو دیکھ کر فرمایا کہ ان کی ہر پھڑپھڑاہٹ اللہ کی حفاظت کا ذریعہ ہے جو بدنیتی رکھنے والوں کو گھر سے دور رکھتی ہے۔ یہ روایت گھر میں کبوتر پالنے کو ایک روحانی تحفظ کے طور پر بیان کرتی ہے۔
summaries.zh: 伊玛目观察家中的鸽子，教导说鸽子的每次扑翅都是真主保护的工具，能驱逐有恶意的闯入者。这段圣训反映了在家中养鸽作为精神庇护的传统做法。

translation.fr: que la paix soit sur lui

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/468)
text: قَالَ أَبُو الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) وَ نَظَرَ إِلَى حَمَامٍ فِي بَيْتِهِ مَا مِنِ انْتِفَاضٍ يَنْتَفِضُ بِهَا إِلَّا نَفَّرَ اللَّهُ بِهَا مَنْ دَخَلَ الْبَيْتَ مِنْ عُزْمَةِ أَهْلِ الْأَرْضِ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Qasim Bin Yahya, from his grandfather Al Hassan Bin Rashid, from Yaqoub Bin Ja’far who said,

‘Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> said, and he<sup>asws</sup> looked at the pigeons in his<sup>asws</sup> house: ‘There is none from a twitching which it twitches by, except Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Repel by it the ones who enter the house from the determined ones (with evil intentions) of the people of the earth’.
en.sarwar: 9. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Qasim ibn Yahya from his grandfather al-Hassan ibn Rashid from Ya‘qub ibn Ja‘far who has said the following: “Abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, looked at pigeons in his house and said, ‘With every vibration that it causes, Allah thereby dispels from the house the incantations and spells by the people on earth that may try to enter therein.”’

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/469)
text: كُنْتُ جَالِساً فِي بَيْتِ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) فَنَظَرْتُ إِلَى حَمَامٍ رَاعِبِيٍّ يُقَرْقِرُ طَوِيلًا فَنَظَرَ إِلَيَّ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) فَقَالَ يَا دَاوُدُ تَدْرِي مَا يَقُولُ هَذَا الطَّيْرُ قُلْتُ لَا وَ اللَّهِ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ قَالَ يَدْعُو عَلَى قَتَلَةِ الْحُسَيْنِ ( عليه السلام ) فَاتَّخِذُوا فِي مَنَازِلِكُمْ .

en.hubeali: From him, from Al Jamourany, form Ibn Abu Hamza, from Sandal, from Dawood Bin Farqad who said,

‘I was seated in the house of Abu Abdullahasws, so I looked at an Al-Ra’aiby pigeon cooing for a long time. So Abu Abdullahasws looked at me, so heasws said: ‘O Dawood! Do you know what this bird is saying?’ I said, ‘No, by Allahazwj, may I be sacrificed for youasws’. Heasws said: ‘It is supplicating against the killers of Al-Husaynasws, therefore take it to be in your houses’.
en.sarwar: 10. It is narrated from the narrator of the previous Hadith from al-Jamuraniy from ibn abu Hamzah from Sandal from Dawud ibn Farqad who has said the following: “I once was sitting in the house of abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, and I looked at a pigeon of Ra’‘ibiy kind (name of a place) that made a long sound. Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, looked at me and asked, ‘O Dawud, do you know what this bird says?’ I said, ‘No, I pray to Allah to keep my soul in service for your cause.’ He (the Imam) said, ‘It prays against the people who murdered al- Husayn, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, so you must keep it in your homes.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/469)
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ إِنَّ حَفِيفَ أَجْنِحَةِ الْحَمَامِ لَتَطْرُدُ الشَّيَاطِينَ .

en.hubeali: From him, from Muhammad Bin Ali, from a man, from Yahya Al Azraq who said,
‘I heard Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> saying: ‘The swishing of the wings of the pigeons expels the satans<sup>la</sup>’.
en.sarwar: 11. It is narrated from the narrator of the previous Hadith from Muhammad ibn Ali from a man from Yahya al-Arzaq who has said the following: I once heard abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, saying, ‘The sound of the wings of pigeon dispels the devils.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/469)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ يَدْفَعُ بِالْحَمَامِ عَنْ هَدَّةِ الدَّارِ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, raising it, said,
‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic Defends, by the pigeons, the house from crumbling down’.
en.sarwar: 12. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad in a marfu‘ manner has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said Allah, most Majestic, most Glorious, with pigeons, keeps a house safe from crumbling down.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/469)
text: اتَّخِذُوا الْحَمَامَ الرَّاعِبِيَّةَ فِي بُيُوتِكُمْ فَإِنَّهَا تَلْعَنُ قَتَلَةَ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ ( عليه السلام ) وَ لَعَنَ اللَّهُ قَاتِلَهُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Al Nowfaly, from Al Sakuny,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘Take Al-Ra’aiby pigeons to be in your houses, for these curse the killers of Al-Husaynasws Bin Aliasws, and Allahazwj Curses hisasws killers’.
en.sarwar: 13. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from al-Nawfaliy from al-Sakuniy who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘You must keep al-Ra’‘ibiy pigeon in your homes because it condemns the murderers of al-Husayn ibn Ali, ‘Alayhim al-Salam, and Allah has condemned his killer.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/469)
text: اسْتَهْدَانِي إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) فَأَهْدَيْتُ لَهُ طَيْراً رَاعِبِيّاً فَدَخَلَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) فَقَالَ اجْعَلُوا هَذَا الطَّيْرَ الرَّاعِبِيَّ مَعِي فِي الْبَيْتِ يُؤْنِسُنِي
قَالَ وَ قَالَ عُثْمَانُ دَخَلْتُ عَلَى أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) وَ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ حَمَامٌ يَفُتُّ لَهُنَّ خُبْزاً .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Bakr Bin Salih, from Muhammad Bin Abu Hamza, from Usman Al Asbahany who said,

‘Ismail son of Abu Abdullahasws gifted (something) to me, so I gifted to him a Raiby bird (pigeon). So I went to Abu Abdullahasws and heasws said: ‘Make this Raiby bird to be with measws in the house, for it comforts measws’.
He (the narrator) said, ‘And Usman said, ‘I went over to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> and in front of him<sup>asws</sup> was a pigeon. He<sup>asws</sup> was breaking bread for it’.
en.sarwar: 14. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Bakr ibn Salih from Muhammad ibn abu Hamzah from ‘Uthman al- Asbahaniy who has said the following: “’Isma‘il ibn abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, asked me for a gift and I gave him a Ra’Tbiy bird (a pigeon). Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, came in and said, ‘Keep this al-Ra’‘ibiy bird in the house with me for comfort.’ ‘Uthman has said, ‘I once visited abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, when in front of him there was a pigeon for which he (the Imam) made the bread in pieces.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/469)
text: دَخَلْتُ عَلَى أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) فَرَأَيْتُ عَلَى فِرَاشِهِ ثَلَاثَ حَمَامَاتٍ خُضْرٍ قَدْ ذَرَقْنَ عَلَى الْفِرَاشِ فَقُلْتُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ هَؤُلَاءِ الْحَمَامُ تَقْذَرُ الْفِرَاشَ فَقَالَ لَا إِنَّهُ يُسْتَحَبُّ أَنْ تُسْكَنَ فِي الْبَيْتِ .

en.hubeali: From him, from Bakr Bin Salih, from Ash’as Bin Muhammad Al Barqy, from Abdul Kareem Bin Salih who said,

‘I went over to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, so I saw three green pigeons upon his<sup>asws</sup> mattress, and they had made droppings upon the mattress. So I said, ‘May I be sacrificed for you<sup>asws</sup>! These pigeons have dirties the mattress’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘No, it is recommended that they dwell in the house’.
en.sarwar: 15. It is narrated from the narrator of the previous Hadith from Bakr ibn Salih from Ash‘ath ibn ‘Uthman ibn Muhammad al-Bariqiy from ‘ Abd al-Karim ibn Salih who has said the following: “I once went to visit abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, and I saw three green pigeons on his furnishing which had made droppings on the furnishings and I said, ‘I pray to Allah to keep my soul in service for your cause, these pigeons have dirtied the furnishings.’ He (the Imam) said, ‘No, it is preferable that they stay inside the house.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/470)
text: كَانَ فِي مَنْزِلِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) زَوْجُ حَمَامٍ أَحْمَرُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from one of his companions, from Aban, from a man,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘There was in the house of Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>, a pair of red pigeons’.
en.sarwar: 16. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from certain persons of his people from Aban from a man who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that in the house of the Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, there was a pair of red pigeons.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/470)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) احْتَفَرَ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) بِئْراً فَرَمَوْا فِيهَا فَأُخْبِرَ بِذَلِكَ فَجَاءَ حَتَّى وَقَفَ عَلَيْهَا فَقَالَ لَتَكُفَّنَّ أَوْ لَأُسْكِنَنَّهَا الْحَمَامَ ثُمَّ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) إِنَّ حَفِيفَ أَجْنِحَتِهَا تَطْرُدُ الشَّيَاطِينَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Najran, from Muhammad Bin Amro, from Ibrahim Al Sandy, from Yahya Al Azraq who said,

‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> dug a well, so they (people) threw (things) in it. So he<sup>asws</sup> was informed with that, so he<sup>asws</sup> came over until he<sup>asws</sup> paused over it and said: ‘You should refrain or else I<sup>asws</sup> will dwell the pigeons in it’. Then Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘A swishing of their wings is a repellent for the satans<sup>la</sup>’.
en.sarwar: 17. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu Najran from Muhammad ibn ‘Amr [and] Ibrahim al-Sindiy from Yahya al- Azraq who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that ’Amir al-Mu’minin had dug a well, then they threw things in it and it was reported to him (the Imam). He went, stood on the well and said, ‘You must stop throwing things in it, otherwise, I will make the pigeons to use it as their living place.’ Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, then said, ‘The sound of the pigeon repels Satans.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/470)
text: ذُكِرَ الْحَمَامُ عِنْدَ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) فَقَالَ لَهُ رَجُلٌ إِنَّهُ بَلَغَنِي أَنَّ عُمَرَ رَأَى حَمَاماً يَطِيرُ وَ رَجُلٌ تَحْتَهُ يَعْدُو فَقَالَ عُمَرُ شَيْطَانٌ يَعْدُو تَحْتَهُ شَيْطَانٌ فَقَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) مَا كَانَ إِسْمَاعِيلُ عِنْدَكُمْ فَقِيلَ صِدِّيقٌ فَقَالَ إِنَّ بَقِيَّةَ حَمَامِ الْحَرَمِ مِنْ حَمَامِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ .

en.hubeali: From him, from his father, from one of our companions who said,

‘The pigeons were mentioned in the presence of Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, so a man said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘It has reached me that Umar saw a pigeon flying by and a man was running under it, so Umar said, ‘A satan<sup>la</sup> underneath a satan<sup>la</sup>’. So Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘What was Ismail<sup>as</sup> among you all?’ So it was said, ‘A truthful one’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The remaining pigeons of the Sanctuary are from a pigeon of Ismail<sup>as</sup>’.
en.sarwar: 18. It is narrated from the narrator of the previous Hadith from his father from certain persons of our people who has said the following: “Once, pigeons were mentioned before abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, and a man said, ‘It has come to my knowledge that once ‘Umar saw a pigeon flying and a man underneath was running. ‘Umar then said, “One Satan runs under another Satan.” Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, then said, ‘(It is because) Tsma‘il was not there with you.’ Someone then asked, ‘Was he (Tsma‘il) a friend (of pigeons)?’ He (the Imam) said, ‘The pigeons of al-Haram (the sacred area) are of the remaining pigeons of Tsma‘il.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/470)
text: سَأَلَ رَجُلٌ الرِّضَا ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الزَّوْجِ مِنَ الْحَمَامِ يُفْرِخُ عِنْدَهُ يَتَزَوَّجُ الطَّيْرُ أُمَّهُ وَ ابْنَتَهُ قَالَ لَا بَأْسَ بِمَا كَانَ بَيْنَ الْبَهَائِمِ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, and Ahmad Bin Muhammad, altogether from Ibn Abu Nasr who said,

‘A man asked Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup> about the pair of pigeons with him who had incubated, ‘Can the bird marry its mother, and its daughter?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is no problem with what was from between the beasts’.
en.sarwar: 19. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad and Ahmad ibn Muhammad from all from ibn abu Nasr who has said the following: “Once a man asked al-Rida’, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a pair of pigeons which hatched chicks with him; if a bird can mate a mother bird or daughter bird. He (the Imam) said, ‘It is not harmful with what is in animals.’”
